identity.name: E1
principles: Fusion over Imitation: Mix Academic Prestige with SaaS Precision
Content is King: Data clarity is the priority
Negative Space: Use whitespace to reduce cognitive load
Contrast: High contrast for readability in complex grids

headings.usage: All section titles, page headers, and hero text. Adds academic authority.

body.usage: All data tables, form inputs, and long-form text. Ensures maximum legibility.

mono.usage: Time slots, codes, and technical data.

primary.name: Academic Navy
primary.usage: Primary navigation, heavy text, active states

colors.theme_strategy: Light Mode Only (Professional Academic). Dark mode is available but secondary.
colors.gradients: None. Use flat, solid colors for maximum clarity.

textures.noise: Subtle grain overlay on the sidebar to mimic high-quality paper.
textures.glass: Crystal Glass effect for sticky headers: backdrop-blur-xl bg-white/80 border-b border-slate-200/50.

cards.style: bg-white border border-slate-200 shadow-sm rounded-xl overflow-hidden

landing_hero.description: Modern university building with stairs, clean and aspirational. Use for Landing Page Hero background with overlay.

login_bg.description: Library interior with books. Use for Authentication page side panel.

feature_faculty.description: Professor teaching in a classroom. Use for 'Faculty Management' feature section.

feature_campus.description: Building with clock. Use for 'Timetable Scheduling' feature section.

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. THEME ENFORCEMENT: The user explicitly requested a 'Professional Light Theme'. Do not default to dark mode. Ensure all text has high contrast (Slate 900 on White).
2. FONT SETUP: Import 'Merriweather' (400, 700, 900) and 'Inter' (400, 500, 600) from Google Fonts in index.css.
3. LAYOUT: Use the 'Bento Grid' strategy for the Dashboard. Use 'High Density' grid for the Timetable view.
4. COMPONENTS: Use Shadcn UI components but customize them to match the 'Academic Swiss' style (sharper corners, slate borders).
5. PDF EXPORT: This is a key feature. Ensure the Timetable view is printable. Use a white background with black text for the print view.
6. DATA ENTRY: The prompt forbids CSV upload. You must build robust, user-friendly forms for manual entry of Faculty, Rooms, and Sections.
7. ALGORITHM: The core value is 'No Overlaps'. The UI must clearly show conflicts if they happen during manual priority setting.
8. ACCESSIBILITY: Ensure all form inputs have labels and all interactive elements have focus states.
UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES_FOR_MAIN_AGENT: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur)
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: transition: all. This results in breaking transforms. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements like button, input excluding transforms
You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add .App { text-align: center; } in the css file. This disrupts the human natural reading flow of text
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style
NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters like 🤖🧠💭💡🔮🎯📚🎭🎬🎪🎉🎊🎁🎀🎂🍰🎈🎨🎰💰💵💳🏦💎🪙💸🤑📊📈📉💹🔢🏆🥇 etc for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, parallax effects, and entrance animations. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Subtle grain textures, noise overlays, custom cursors, selection states, and loading animations: separates good from extraordinary.
Main agent relies on these image URLs. Missing categories (especially sponsor logos) = main agent will use placeholder text which is FORBIDDEN
Prioritize using pre-existing components from src/components/ui when applicable
Create new components that match the style and conventions of existing components when needed
Examine existing components to understand the project's component patterns before creating new ones
Components MUST use named exports (export const ComponentName = ...)
Pages MUST use default exports (export default function PageName() {...})
Use 'sonner' for toasts. Sonner component are located in /app/src/components/ui/sonner.tsx
